Home / Services / Driveway Installation

Driveway Installation

Creating durable and stylish driveways that enhance your curb appeal.

A well-designed and properly installed driveway can significantly enhance the curb appeal and value of your Nashville home. Cardena's Hard Landscaping specializes in creating driveways that are both durable and aesthetically pleasing. We understand the importance of proper drainage and base preparation, ensuring that your driveway will withstand heavy traffic and Nashville's weather conditions.

We offer a variety of driveway materials, including asphalt, concrete, pavers, and gravel, to suit your style and budget. Our experienced team handles all aspects of the installation process, from excavation and grading to paving and sealing. We pay close attention to detail, ensuring that your driveway is smooth, level, and properly sloped for drainage.

Whether you're looking to replace an old, cracked driveway or create a new driveway for a new construction project, Cardena's Hard Landscaping can provide a solution that meets your needs. We're committed to providing exceptional quality and customer service, ensuring that your driveway is a durable and attractive addition to your Nashville property.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Design & Material Selection
  2. 02 Step 2: Excavation & Grading
  3. 03 Step 3: Base Preparation & Compaction
  4. 04 Step 4: Driveway Installation & Sealing

Benefits

  • Increased property value
  • Enhanced curb appeal
  • Durable and long-lasting
  • Improved drainage

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best material for a driveway in Nashville?

Concrete and asphalt are popular choices due to their durability and affordability. Pavers offer a more aesthetically pleasing look but are typically more expensive. Gravel is a budget-friendly option but requires more maintenance.

How thick should a driveway be?

A residential driveway should typically be at least 4 inches thick to withstand the weight of vehicles. For heavier vehicles or commercial applications, a thicker driveway may be required.

How often should a driveway be sealed?

Asphalt driveways should be sealed every 2-3 years to protect them from the elements and prevent cracking. Concrete driveways can be sealed less frequently, typically every 3-5 years.
